title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
text_en: With the grafts securely in place, your surgeon will use electrical signals to restore the heart beat and attach a temporary pacemaker to the heart. Once your heart is again beating normally, the heart lung machine will be disconnected.

file: ANS00254_Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed.mp4
title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
text_en: Your surgeon will wire the breastbone back together and suture the skin incision closed. A temporary drainage tube will be placed through the skin beneath the incision.

title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
text_en: Off-pump cabbages also called beating heart bypass grafting because the heart isn't stopped and a heart lung machine isn't used. Instead a mechanical device is used to steady the part of the heart where grafting is being

file: ANS00254_Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed.mp4
title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
text_en: Students perform minimally invasive bypass procedures using specially designed instruments inserted through small incisions or ports in the chest. These procedures sometimes require a heart lung machine.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Atherosclerosis is a life-threatening disease that may have begun to develop during childhood. This condition is a process in which deposits of fatty material, called plaque, build up inside the walls of arteries, reducing or completely blocking blood flow.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Although the exact causes of atherosclerosis are not clear, many scientists think it begins with damage to the inner wall of an artery, called the endothelium, substances traveling in the blood, such as cholesterol, fats, and cellular waste products accumulate inside the damaged area of the arterial wall.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Chemical reactions occurring within the build up of material cause cholesterol molecules to oxidize. This initiates an inflammatory response in which the endothelial cells, at the damage site, release chemicals that signal a call for help.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: In response, monocytes from the bloodstream travel to the damaged site. Stimulation from oxidized cholesterol converts the monocytes into macrophages.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: The macrophages eat and digest the cholesterol molecules. As a result of this process, the macrophages change into foam cells, which accumulate to form plaque.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: As the plaque increases in size, the arterial wall thickens and hardens. At the same time, smooth muscle cells within the arterial wall begin to multiply. Most of the smooth muscle cells move to the surface of the plaque. These cells contribute to the formation of a firm, fibrous cap covering the plaque.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Eventually, the passageway through the artery narrows, enough to reduce blood flow, and the amount of oxygen received by the organs it supplies.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Over time, the cap may erode and break open, releasing plaque into the bloodstream. The plaque can flow downstream and contribute to the formation of a blood clot, which can stop blood flow. As a result, limited blood supply is available to the area surrounding the partially blocked artery, degrading, and potentially killing the neighboring tissue.

title: ANS00339 Electrocardiography (ECG)
text_en: Sticky pads will be placed on your chest to check your heart rate.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will perform an abdominal aortic aneurysm repair to prevent the rupture of a bulging area of your aorta. Open abdominal aortic aneurysm repair.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making an incision from the breastbone to below the navel.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: He or she will then locate the aneurysm and clamp the aorta just above and below it.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will then open the aneurysm and sew an artificial graft the same size and shape as a healthy aorta into place.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will stitch the wall of the aneurysm closed over the newly placed graft. The graft will reinforce the walls of the aorta.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will close the abdominal incision with stitches.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: The total length of this procedure is usually 4 to 6 hours.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The largest artery in the body is the aorta, which is attached to the heart and receives blood directly from the left ventricle or main pumping chamber.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The aorta is divided into four parts, the ascending aorta, aortic arch, thoracic aorta, and the abdominal aorta.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The abdominal aorta is the last and largest section of the aorta. It supplies blood to the lower regions of your body, including the organs and the abdomen and pelvis.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The abdominal aorta ends around naval level, where it splits into the two iliac arteries that carry blood to the legs.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will perform an abdominal aortic aneurysm repair to prevent the rupture of a bulging area of your aorta, endovascular aneurysm repair.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making a small incision in your groin over your femoral artery. Then he or she will insert a guide wire into the artery and gently push it up toward the site of your aneurysm.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: A catheter or hollow tube will be passed over the guide wire and pushed toward your aneurysm.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Using live x-ray pictures as a guide, your surgeon will pass an endovascular stent graft, a compressed fabric and metal tube, through the catheter to the aneurysm. Then he or she will open the graft which will be held in place with metallic hooks and stents.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon may insert additional graft components to extend the graft into the blood vessels supplying your legs.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Once the graft is in place, your blood will flow through it, not into the surrounding aneurysm sac, which will remove the pressure from your aneurysm.
